column1: # PRE-1978 ONLY. For 1978-2026 the eligible (citizen voting-age) figure is
# PUBLISHED by the CA Secretary of State - use data/sf_registration_eligible.csv
# These rows are ROUGH citizen-eligible estimates for the years the SoS published
# no county eligible figure (pre-1973). eligible_citizen_est = total_population x
# an assumed citizen-of-total share. The share is anchored to the SoS-vs-census
# ratio where both exist: citizen-eligible was ~80% of all adults in 1990 and
# ~72% of total population in 1980 -> SF has a large non-citizen adult share
# eligible is well BELOW all-adult voting-age population. Pre-1971 voting age was
# 21+. These are assumption-based; exact figures need Census citizenship tables
# (NHGIS / decennial volumes). Totals are sourced (decennial census).

column5: census 1900 total; eligible est = total x 0.55 (assumed; 21+, high immigrant/non-citizen share)
census 1970 total; x 0.65 (assumed; 21+, voting age -> 18 in 1971)
SOV 1976 printed VAP 535,094 (all adults) x ~0.88 citizen share = ~471k eligible
